猜想.摘要: Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.72) and top2 is ICT (0.10). The latest evidence strengthens the current top1 because the exact donor-acceptor layout screen directly supports weak donor-acceptor segregation and aromatic-core dominance, which is more consistent with a neutral-aromatic/locally excited picture than with strong ICT. The top2 challenger ICT is weakened by this same evidence because it loses structural support for meaningful charge separation, and prior CT-localization parsing had already failed to provide positive ICT-specific support. As an additional candidate, TICT stays low but not zero: many rotors preserve residual plausibility, yet it remains capability-blocked and gains no new positive support from the latest evidence. No still-credible hypothesis remains in coverage debt, so the case has legitimately entered and remains in pairwise contraction rather than portfolio screening. The key remaining discriminator is no longer a new screening observable; it is whether the existing mixed internal-plus-external record is sufficient for best-available closure despite the lack of exact-identity literature and direct twisting observables. Given round 7 of 20 with 14 rounds still available, the best next action is to hold pairwise contraction and mark the case ready for best-available finalization rather than spend another round on lower-yield repeated supplementation.

The latest evidence strengthens the current top1 because the exact donor-acceptor layout screen directly supports weak donor-acceptor segregation and aromatic-core dominance, which is more consistent with a neutral-aromatic/locally excited picture than with strong ICT. The top2 challenger ICT is weakened by this same evidence because it loses structural support for meaningful charge separation, and prior CT-localization parsing had already failed to provide positive ICT-specific support. As an additional candidate, TICT stays low but not zero: many rotors preserve residual plausibility, yet it remains capability-blocked and gains no new positive support from the latest evidence. No still-credible hypothesis remains in coverage debt, so the case has legitimately entered and remains in pairwise contraction rather than portfolio screening. The key remaining discriminator is no longer a new screening observable; it is whether the existing mixed internal-plus-external record is sufficient for best-available closure despite the lack of exact-identity literature and direct twisting observables. Given round 7 of 20 with 14 rounds still available, the best next action is to hold pairwise contraction and mark the case ready for best-available finalization rather than spend another round on lower-yield repeated supplementation.

Verifier supplementation for neutral aromatic versus ICT is already sufficient and informative at the close-family level. It supports the current top hypothesis more than ICT but is not exact-identity evidence.

Closure justification is sufficient for the active pair because the previously missing exact internal donor-acceptor layout discriminator has now been obtained and it aligns with already sufficient verifier support favoring neutral aromatic over ICT.

Portfolio screening remains complete. All non-leading hypotheses are already directly screened, blocked, or dropped. No new coverage debt was created by the latest evidence; instead, the active neutral aromatic versus ICT pair has now received the missing exact internal discriminator.

Current capabilities have been used efficiently. Microscopic baseline work supplied compatible state-ordering evidence but repeatedly failed to expose decisive CT or torsion observables on parse routes. Macro screening successfully delivered the exact bounded structural discriminator needed for the active pair. Verifier supplementation is already sufficient and informative, though not exact-identity specific. Another round of the same routes would likely have lower incremental value than synthesis and closure.

Latest exact macro evidence: donor-acceptor layout screen found weak donor-acceptor segregation and aromatic-core dominance. Prior internal evidence: baseline S0/S1 observations were compatible with a neutral-aromatic or locally excited picture; CT/localization parse was nondiscriminative; twisting observables were unavailable. External evidence: verifier supplementation for neutral aromatic versus ICT is sufficient, high-information, and overall more supportive of neutral aromatic than ICT, though based on close-family rather than exact-identity precedent.
